blocks.text: This Data Processing Agreement (“DPA”) forms part of the agreement or terms under which Kanbanify provides the Services to a Customer (the “Main Agreement”). This DPA applies where Kanbanify processes Personal Data on behalf of the Customer as a Processor. This DPA is intended to be jurisdiction neutral while supporting GDPR-aligned processing standards.

items: Controller, Processor, Personal Data, Data Subject, Processing, Personal Data

blocks.text: Breach: as defined in applicable Data Protection Laws.

items: Data Protection Laws means all applicable laws and regulations relating to privacy

blocks.text: or the Processing of Personal Data, including where applicable the GDPR and other comparable privacy frameworks.

items: Services means the Kanbanify platform made available via kanbanify.org and any

blocks.text: related subdomains, including collaboration features such as boards, chat, meetings, documents, and reporting.

items: Sub-processor means any third party engaged by Kanbanify to Process Personal

blocks.text: Data on behalf of the Customer.

a. The Customer is the Controller of Customer Personal Data. b. Kanbanify is the Processor of Customer Personal Data to the extent Kanbanify processes such data on behalf of Customer in providing the Services.

sections.title: Where Kanbanify processes Personal Data for its own independent purposes (for

blocks.text: example, account administration and security operations), Kanbanify acts as a Controller for that limited Processing.
Kanbanify will Process Personal Data only as needed to provide the Services under the Main Agreement and in accordance with Customer instructions.
Subject matter
Provision of project management and collaboration Services, including boards, tasks, chat, meetings, documents, and reporting exports.
Nature and purpose

items: Create and manage workspaces and user access
Enable collaboration on tasks and projects
Support chat and meeting features
Store and retrieve documents and attachments
Generate reporting outputs, including manual timesheet entry and report exports
Operate, maintain, secure, and support the Services

blocks.text: Duration
Processing continues for the duration of the Main Agreement, and thereafter for the period needed to complete deletion or return obligations, subject to legally required retention.
Categories of Data Subjects

items: Customer users and invited users, including employees, contractors, or

blocks.text: collaborators authorised by Customer
Types of Personal Data
Depending on Customer configuration and use of the Services:

items: Account and profile data (name, email, username, role, workspace membership)
Authentication data (password hash, session identifiers)
Workspace content entered by users:

blocks.text: o Tasks, comments, descriptions, assignments o Chat messages o Meeting details, including scheduling metadata and participant lists o Meeting recordings and meeting transcripts where enabled o Documents, attachments, and wiki-style content

items: Reporting data:

blocks.text: o Manual timesheet entries and report exports (XLS, XLSX)

items: Technical and security data:

blocks.text: o IP address and security logs (see retention in Annex 3)
Customer will:

sections.title: Ensure it has a valid legal basis to collect and provide Personal Data to Kanbanify.
Provide any required notices to Data Subjects and obtain consents where required.
Ensure Customer instructions comply with Data Protection Laws.
Manage user access, roles, and permissions appropriately, including administrative

blocks.text: access to workspace content.
Kanbanify will, with respect to Customer Personal Data:
Processing on instructions
Process Customer Personal Data only:

items: as necessary to provide the Services, and
in accordance with Customer documented instructions,

blocks.text: unless required by law to process otherwise. If legally permitted, Kanbanify will notify Customer before Processing under such legal requirement.
Confidentiality
Ensure personnel authorised to Process Customer Personal Data are bound by confidentiality obligations.
Security measures
Implement appropriate technical and organisational measures (“TOMs”) to protect Customer Personal Data, described in Annex 1.
Sub-processors
Engage Sub-processors only in accordance with Section 7.
Data Subject requests
If Kanbanify receives a request directly from a Data Subject relating to Customer Personal Data, Kanbanify will:

items: notify Customer, and
not respond unless instructed by Customer, except as required by law.

blocks.text: Personal Data Breach notification
Notify Customer without undue delay after becoming aware of a Personal Data Breach affecting Customer Personal Data.
Return or deletion
Upon termination or expiry of the Main Agreement, Kanbanify will delete or return Customer Personal Data in accordance with Annex 2, unless retention is required by law.
Audit assistance
Provide reasonable information necessary to demonstrate compliance with this DPA. If Customer requests an audit, it must be:

items: limited in scope to Processing under this DPA,
subject to reasonable notice and scheduling,
conducted in a manner that avoids disruption to other customers and protects

blocks.text: confidentiality and security.

sections.title: Customer instructions should be documented (for example, via configuration

blocks.text: settings, administrator actions, or written instructions).

sections.title: If Kanbanify reasonably believes an instruction violates Data Protection Laws,

blocks.text: Kanbanify may pause the instruction and notify Customer.

sections.title: Customer grants Kanbanify general authorisation to appoint Sub-processors to

blocks.text: provide the Services.

sections.title: Kanbanify will require Sub-processors to implement data protection obligations

blocks.text: that provide a level of protection appropriate to the Services.

sections.title: Kanbanify will remain responsible for Sub-processor performance of obligations

blocks.text: relating to Processing under this DPA.

sections.title: Processing may occur globally depending on infrastructure location, resilience,

blocks.text: and operational needs.

sections.title: Where Data Protection Laws require safeguards for international transfers,

blocks.text: Kanbanify will implement appropriate transfer mechanisms consistent with applicable requirements.

sections.title: Encryption is enabled across the Services by default.
Workspace roles may enable visibility of linked task context, including related chat

blocks.text: and meeting information, consistent with the platform design.

a. Where recordings and transcripts are available, they are accessible to participants of the relevant meeting or group, including participants who did not attend, similar to standard collaboration tools. b. Customer controls who is a participant and who has workspace access.
Retention is described in Annex 3, including:

items: IP log retention: 30 days
Other retention periods where applicable or required for security, operational

blocks.text: integrity, or legal compliance
Liability under this DPA will follow the liability terms in the Main Agreement and any mandatory provisions under applicable Data Protection Laws.
This DPA remains in effect for the duration of the Main Agreement and applies to Processing performed during that period.
Privacy and data protection enquiries relating to this DPA: support@kanbanify.org
Measures Kanbanify will maintain measures appropriate to the nature of the Services and the risks, which may include:

items: Role-based access controls for workspaces
Administrative access restricted to authorised roles
Least-privilege internal access controls where applicable
Encryption in transit (TLS)
Encryption enabled across the Services by default
Controls to prevent unauthorised access to decrypted content
Passwords stored as salted, hashed values
Session controls and secure cookie handling for authenticated access
Security logging to detect abuse and operational issues
Access controls around logs
Backups and recovery procedures appropriate to the Services
Measures designed to restore availability following an incident
Patch management and security updates
Reasonable vulnerability handling processes

sections.title: Procedures to assess, contain, and remediate incidents

blocks.text: Termination

sections.title: Upon termination or expiry of the Main Agreement, Kanbanify will delete or return

blocks.text: Customer Personal Data within a commercially reasonable period consistent with standard SaaS practice and operational constraints.

sections.title: If deletion is not immediately feasible due to backups or technical constraints,

blocks.text: Kanbanify will:

items: isolate data from active Processing where practical, and
complete deletion during normal backup rotation cycles where applicable.

sections.title: Kanbanify may retain limited data where required by law or for the establishment,

blocks.text: exercise, or defence of legal claims.

sections.title: IP logs and security logs: 30 days
Workspace content and files: retained while the workspace remains active,

blocks.text: unless deleted by Customer users or administrators, subject to any technical constraints for deletion completion

sections.title: Account data: retained while the account remains active, and thereafter as needed

blocks.text: to complete deletion and comply with legal or security obligations

sections.title: Meeting recordings and transcripts: retained while enabled and stored within the

blocks.text: workspace scope, accessible to meeting participants, and subject to Customer deletion actions and technical constraints for deletion completion
